title: Meetra Surik's Jedi Master

{{Quote|Today I caught her in a heated argument with my Padawan! Her Master refuses to properly discipline… (static) …I want to know what action you intend!|Vrook Lamar, to Vandar Tokare, in a conversation recorded by a protocol droid regarding an argument between Meetra Surik and Lamar's Padawan|<ref name="KotOR II game">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>}}
A '''Jedi''' who was a [[Jedi/Legends|member]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] in the [[Time/Legends|time]] period prior to the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. That Jedi was the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] of the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Meetra Surik]] and [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] her on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], where a [[Jedi Enclave]] was located. The Master refused to take any action when Surik argued with Jedi Master [[Vrook Lamar]]'s [[Vrook Lamar's Padawan|Padawan]]. Surik eventually left the Master's tutelage and rebelled against the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] by participating in the [[Mandalorian Wars]], which was the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] conflict with the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]].

==Biography==
===Argument with Meetra Surik===
[[File:JediExileTOR.png|thumb|right|180px|The Jedi trained Meetra Surik in the ways of the Jedi Order.]]
A [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] lived around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Mandalorian Wars]] and [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]]<ref name="Holovid" /> [[Meetra Surik]]<ref name="Note" /> as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], which was the location of a [[Jedi Enclave]]. After Surik had been in a heated argument with [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vrook Lamar]]'s [[Vrook Lamar's Padawan|Padawan]], Lamar came to Surik's Master and asked that she be disciplined for her actions. The Master refused, forcing Lamar to seek out Master [[Vandar Tokare]] to discipline Surik, who also refused, citing that the matter was not Lamar's concern.<ref name="Holovid" /> Surik eventually left her Master's tutelage and rebelled against the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] by joining the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak|Malak's]] [[Revanchist|faction]] of Jedi in the [[Mandalorian Wars]], a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-spanning conflict between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]].<ref name="KotOR II game" />

===Legacy===
{{Gamemechanics}}
During the [[Dark Wars]] in [[3951 BBY]],<ref name="KotOR CG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> Surik—who was attempting to track down and reunite the Jedi Masters who had survived the [[Sith Triumvirate]]'s [[First Jedi Purge|campaign]] against the remnants of the Jedi Order—located and repaired a [[battered protocol droid]] near the [[Khoonda]] outpost on Dantooine after disembarking her starship ''[[Ebon Hawk]]''. The droid replayed a [[Holovid/Legends|holorecording]] of a discussion between Jedi Masters Lamar and [[Vandar Tokare]] regarding the argument between the former's Padawan and Surik and the lack of disciplinary action from Surik's Master.<ref name="Holovid" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
Surik's Master did not feel that the argument between Lamar's Padawan and Surik was notable enough for disciplinary action, despite Lamar's objections to Surik's Master.<ref name="Holovid" />

==Powers and abilities==
Surik's Master was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|sensitive]] to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], and advanced to the point where the Jedi was capable of training Surik in the ways of the Jedi Order.<ref name="Holovid" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Jedi was first mentioned in the [[2004]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', developed by [[Obsidian Entertainment]]. If the player character, known in-game as the Exile, repaired a battered protocol droid outside of [[Khoonda]] on the planet Dantooine, the droid showed a [[Hologram/Legends|holorecording]] of a conversation between Vrook Lamar and Vandar Tokare regarding the protagonist. During this dialogue, the character's Jedi Master is mentioned. The ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' stated that the Exile had several informal masters before the Mandalorian Wars, including [[Kavar]] and [[Vima Sunrider]]. The novel ''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]'' confirmed the name of the Jedi Master's Padawan, the protagonist of ''The Sith Lords'', as Meetra Surik.<ref name="Note" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{1stm}}
